question: Can the paper proceed without a positive adapted-model claim?
trigger: Steps15-25 still block positive model-dependent evidence
method_sketch: Write final synthesis that claims tokenizer bottleneck, clean tokenizer/init artifacts, added-token failure diagnosis, smaller-vocab redesign evidence, and longer-budget failure only; remove downstream/translation success wording
required_gate: No unsupported positive claim remains in claim_evidence_map
notes: No new model experiment is required for a negative/diagnostic framing

question: Can added-token prediction be learned without damaging base/all loss?
trigger: Step17 localizes failure to added tokens; Steps18-21 fail to repair it; Step23 tests smaller vocab
method_sketch: Use train/MAR-dev only; test tokenizer-size/model tradeoff such as 8k/16k/32k or an objective schedule that controls added-token frequency and base-token preservation
required_gate: added_loss improves 3/3; base_loss nonworse 3/3; all_loss nonworse 3/3; trainable and no-final audits pass
notes: Step23 passes with 8k and 16k; best 8k raw mean final loss 4.541285 vs 32k 4.946829

question: Does a repaired adapted checkpoint beat or match original continued pretraining fairly?
trigger: Step24 and Step25 show selected/longer 8k branch still fails normalized control
method_sketch: Rerun Step15-style adapted vs original-control training with matched train token budget and at least 3 seeds only after a new objective/data redesign
required_gate: adapted/original raw and normalized ratios <=1.100000; token budget ratio <=1.020000
notes: Step25 longer-budget word/char normalized ratio is 1.587381, so downstream/translation remain blocked

question: Does the repaired model improve nontrivial encoder-only tasks?
trigger: E02 passes and model is frozen
method_sketch: Use hard-negative retrieval/matching with bootstrap confidence intervals; select only on train/MAR-dev before final
required_gate: Seed/language gains are statistically supported and not driven by language ID
notes: Do not start as top-tier positive evidence before MLM/control gate passes

question: Does adapted XLM-R reach 80 percent of high-resource reference under the same method?
trigger: E02 passes and pair/scoring are frozen on MAR-dev
method_sketch: Run adapted-XLM-R-only method-matched retrieval or generation; read ACT final exactly once
required_gate: target/high-resource ratio >=0.800000 on ACT clean final

question: Does the result generalize beyond Bible-domain text?
trigger: Any broad low-resource generalization wording is desired
method_sketch: Add a non-Bible external domain with train/dev/final separation
required_gate: Directional gains hold outside Bible domain
notes: Needed only for broad domain wording

question: Is the successful repair robust to initialization choice?
trigger: E01 and E02 produce a successful objective
method_sketch: Rerun random, mean, fvt, align, and focus under the successful repair objective
required_gate: at least selected method remains best or differences are explained across 3 seeds
notes: Step14 covers zero-step all methods; Step21 covers full-budget mean/align only
